name: Taylor County Bank
description_short: Taylor County Bank is a locally-owned community bank in Kentucky serving Taylor County since 1937, offering personal checking, savings, CDs, and debit card products.
description_long:
Taylor County Bank was founded on September 1, 1937, by J.C. Miller Sr., a builder and engineer who envisioned creating a financial institution in the aftermath of the Great Depression when most local banks had failed. With $300,000 in capital, the bank was established on the principle of personal, one-on-one service and keeping commitments—values described as rooted in "earnest effort and sincerity" where "a contract and a handshake was considered a sacred bond." The bank has remained under Miller family leadership through four generations, with the current president being Jim Miller.

Taylor County Bank offers a range of consumer banking products including checking and savings accounts, flexible Add-To Certificates of Deposit (CDs), and debit card services. Their Savings Round-Up feature automatically rounds debit card purchases to the nearest dollar and transfers the difference to savings. They also provide Kids Debit Cards to help children learn financial management. The bank emphasizes accessibility through online banking and customer support via phone, email, and in-person visits at their headquarters location.

What distinguishes Taylor County Bank is its singular status as the only locally-owned and operated financial institution in Taylor County. The bank has deep community roots spanning over 80 years and positions itself around personalized service and financial literacy initiatives for the community. Leadership has explicitly committed to providing "the level of service we would want for our friends and our families," differentiating themselves from regional or national competitors.

As a community bank, Taylor County Bank appears well-established and legitimate, but the available information is limited to basic product offerings. The website does not provide details on current interest rates, fees, minimum balances, CD terms, or competitive comparisons with regional alternatives. Customers should contact the bank directly at (270) 465-4196 or support@taylorcountybank.com for specific product terms and pricing. The bank's geographic focus on Taylor County may limit convenience for those outside the area.
services: Online banking login and account access
Financial literacy programs and community education
Account transfer assistance for new customers
pros: Only locally-owned and operated bank in Taylor County with 80+ year history since 1937
Savings Round-Up feature automatically transfers rounded debit card purchases to savings without manual effort
Add-To CDs allow flexible deposit additions at any time rather than lump-sum only contributions
Kids Debit Cards help children build healthy financial habits and learn money management early
Community-focused with dedicated financial literacy programs for the community
Personal one-on-one service model emphasized by leadership and founding principles
Multiple contact methods available: phone (270) 465-4196, email, and in-person visits
cons: No publicly available information on current interest rates, APYs, or CD terms on website
Limited geographic reach—primarily serves Taylor County area, inconvenient for customers outside the region
Website does not display fee schedules, minimum balance requirements, or account opening details
No mention of online account opening, mobile app, or digital banking capabilities beyond login reference
Limited product range compared to regional/national banks—no mention of mortgages, business loans, credit cards, or wealth management
best_for: Taylor County residents seeking personal relationships with their bank and community-focused service
Parents teaching children financial management through the Kids Debit Cards program
Savers interested in flexible CD options that allow additional deposits over time
Individuals prioritizing support for locally-owned institutions over national chains
diagnosis: Taylor County Bank is best for individuals in Taylor County seeking traditional community banking with personalized service and values-based operations. The main caveat is the lack of publicly available rate and fee information, limited digital banking details, and geographic constraint to Taylor County—potential customers should contact the bank directly to compare products and pricing before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
